milestones.title: Planning and contract freeze
milestones.summary: Contract docs, module matrix, bring-up notes, and rollout plan exist in nursery and are sufficient to keep implementation scoped.

evidence.description: Primary milestone and acceptance plan.
Track A selector, kill switch, denylist, and fallback contract.
Shared module matrix for Track A and Track B.

exitCriteria: Runtime seam contract is frozen enough for implementation slices.
Gate mapping and rollback policy are documented.
Track B module boundaries are drafted before code promotion.
nextActions: Keep all milestone status changes in this manifest instead of scattered notes.
Update the manifest when any milestone changes state.

milestones.title: Track A prototype (flagged, fallback first)
milestones.summary: Local seam bring-up evidence exists and fresh host validation now confirms both Dawn and Doe pass the smoke harness with the nursery wrappers resolving the working browser path by default.

evidence.description: Bring-up flow and lane wrapper usage.
Track A selector and decoder seam work log.
Local Chromium lane bring-up and selector status snapshot.
Lane dependency and resolved-path preflight.
Linux bring-up wrapper for Chromium lane setup/build.
Latest local browser-layered summary artifact showing Doe vs Dawn execution in the lane.
Fresh host smoke artifact showing Dawn and Doe both pass browser WebGPU smoke.

exitCriteria: Flagged Doe selection launches Chromium workloads without changing Dawn-default behavior.
Fallback reasons are observable and deterministic.
Unsupported or denylisted configurations fail closed to Dawn unless Doe is explicitly forced.
blockers: Chromium seam edits still live in the lane checkout and are not promoted into governed core directories.
nextActions: Keep selector/fallback evidence current in lane-local artifacts.
Keep the working host app and nursery default path resolution in sync with the Chromium lane output.

milestones.title: Track A compatibility hardening
milestones.summary: The browser benchmark superset, workflow manifests, and checker now have fresh local evidence: the strict 80-row layered browser run passes with zero required failures on both Dawn and Doe.

evidence.description: L0/L1/L2 browser projection and promotion contract.
Generated browser projection manifest from core workloads.
L2 browser workflow definitions.
Projection and coverage checker for browser-layered runs.
Latest strict local browser-layered checker artifact for the current 80-row projection.
Fresh March 8 browser-lane status note with strict-run outcomes.

exitCriteria: Required L1 and L2 browser rows report explicit typed status and statusCode.
Projection and workflow hashes stay synchronized with core workloads.
Trace and compatibility evidence can be refreshed by script rather than manual curation.
blockers: Browser-layered evidence is still nursery-local and not yet promotion-grade compatibility proof.
CTS/browser-suite coverage remains referenced in planning docs but is not yet wired as a required nursery artifact.
nextActions: Add explicit CTS/browser-suite artifact paths when those runs exist.
Promote strict browser-layered validation from nursery-local artifacts into a governed compatibility lane.

milestones.title: Track A performance qualification
milestones.summary: Fresh browser-layered and smoke artifacts exist for the current projection manifest, and they correctly classify browser performance as diagnostic rather than claimable.

evidence.description: One-command browser-layered benchmark wrapper.
Generate-run-check-summary orchestration for the browser lane.
Latest browser-layered performance summary; still diagnostic by contract.
Fresh smoke performance proxy artifact; still diagnostic by contract.

exitCriteria: Browser reports state diagnostic vs claimable status explicitly.
No browser-layered artifact is misrepresented as an L0 release claim.
Timing source and comparability scope stay explicit in generated summaries.
blockers: The current browser summaries are directional/diagnostic, not claimable.
Strict browser-level claim lanes need more methodology and workload-specific evidence.
No browser release-claim gate is satisfied by the current artifacts.
nextActions: Keep browser artifacts out of canonical claim paths unless formally promoted.
Define an explicit browser claim lane only if timing-scope symmetry and release claimability requirements can be met.

milestones.title: Track B module 1 (fawn_2d_sdf_renderer) incubation
milestones.summary: The first Track B module now has a schema-backed deterministic prototype lane with request fixtures, config-driven policy, and a passing gate report.

evidence.description: Candidate input/output/fallback contract for the first Track B module.
Config-driven thresholds and allowed operation policy for Track B prototypes.
Schema-backed request/result contract for the SDF renderer prototype.
Deterministic SDF renderer prototype request fixture.
Prototype gate for all Track B module fixtures.
Passing Track B prototype gate report with deterministic fixture results.

exitCriteria: A deterministic fixture lane exists for the SDF renderer.
Fallback reasons are typed and traceable.
CPU-owned semantics remain outside the module boundary.
blockers: The current SDF lane is a deterministic prototype and not a real GPU-backed Chromium module implementation.
No promotion-ready core integration or correctness corpus exists beyond the prototype fixture.
nextActions: Extend the fixture corpus beyond the single deterministic request before claiming coverage.
Replace the prototype logic with real GPU-backed execution before any promotion discussion.

milestones.title: Track B additional modules (one-by-one)
milestones.summary: All additional Track B modules now have schema-backed deterministic prototype fixtures and pass the shared Track B prototype gate.

evidence.description: Path engine contract draft.
Effects pipeline contract draft.
Compute services contract draft.
Resource scheduler contract draft.
Schema-backed path engine request/result contract.
Schema-backed effects pipeline request/result contract.
Schema-backed compute services request/result contract.
Schema-backed resource scheduler request/result contract.
Deterministic path engine prototype request fixture.
Deterministic effects pipeline prototype request fixture.
Deterministic compute services prototype request fixture.
Deterministic resource scheduler prototype request fixture.
Shared deterministic Track B prototype runner.
Passing Track B prototype gate report covering all additional modules.

exitCriteria: Each module has an independent artifact lane and kill switch.
No module skips schema/correctness/trace gates.
Each module reports claimable vs diagnostic status explicitly.
blockers: These additional modules are deterministic prototypes, not real Chromium module integrations.
No per-module correctness corpus, trace corpus, or browser wiring exists beyond the prototype fixtures.
nextActions: Promote Track B modules one at a time only after replacing the prototypes with real execution paths.
Add richer per-module fixture corpora and explicit fallback fixtures before any performance discussion.

milestones.title: Promotion and maintenance
milestones.summary: Promotion governance exists and Track B now has executable prototype evidence, but nothing has been migrated out of nursery or accepted into core maintenance yet.

evidence.description: Promotion approval artifact schema.
Explicit promotion approvals state.

exitCriteria: Promoted behavior is no longer dependent on nursery-only assumptions.
Required gate wiring is defined in core directories.
Rollback and ownership are explicit before promotion.
blockers: Approvals are intentionally ungranted and there is no promoted browser scope yet.
No Track B prototype has been rewritten as a core-runtime implementation with config/migration/status updates.
nextActions: Use this milestone only after specific Track A or Track B slices are promotion-ready.
Do not treat local prototype evidence as promotion approval.
